prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal editorial illustration that explains ONE idea: "Controlling garden irrigation via Home Assistant."

Composition (contraption): Mascot "vinfelt" watering a small layered felt house using a simple felt hose connected to a valve. Generous negative space (keep ~35%+ of the canvas empty); the subject is large and confident, ~50–70% of the frame.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot — a plump cat-like blob figure built from layered hand-cut felt pieces (a smooth, rounded dome body with two tiny triangular felt ears on top), two printed dot eyes, blank deadpan (no eyebrows, no mouth), small stubby felt arms and legs; the ONLY accent-colored part is the tail (a small curved felt tail at the back). The mascot is itself built of stacked hand-cut felt layers exactly like every other felt piece in the scene. It MUST perform the move, not decorate. In a light palette the body felt is pale/oat and the eyes are structure-ink dots.

LINE LANGUAGE: build EVERYTHING — mascot, objects, props — from layered hand-cut FELT pieces with soft rounded edges and a visible fuzzy nap; NO drawn outlines — shapes separate by flat felt color, by the soft drop shadow where one felt layer sits on another, and by occasional simple stitch dashes; the mascot's body itself reads as stacked felt layers (rows of feathers, quills, petals, or tufts), built exactly like every other felt piece in the scene.

STYLE: LAYERED FELT-CRAFT diorama — matte wool-felt cutouts stacked in shallow layers on a felt ground, soft fuzzy fiber texture on every surface, gentle soft drop shadows between stacked layers, slightly imperfect hand-cut edges and small handmade misalignment; NOT glossy, NOT a 3D render, no plastic sheen, no photorealistic depth-of-field, no gradients within a piece (one flat felt color per shape).

PALETTE: a soft felt ground #e7e2d2 breathing through. Layered felt in a small craft color set: body oat #c9bfa6, secondary sage #8fa682, clay #c08a6e; structure-ink #3a352e only for the eyes, nose, mouth and fine detail. Accent #ff3d9a used sparingly — the character's one focal accent part (its tail). One flat felt color per shape; soft drop shadows only between stacked layers.

LABELS: exactly 2 short hand-lettered English labels — {"HASS", "IRRIGATE"} — in the structure-ink color placed directly on the bare felt ground; slightly irregular, stitched look. Never put label text on a colored fill. No title bar, no type label, no logo.


Aspect ratio: 16:9.

A 16:9 horizontal editorial illustration that explains ONE idea: "Tracking visited cities around the world."

Composition (diorama): Mascot "vinfelt" sitting and pointing at a spot on a large felt globe model. Next to vinfelt is a tiny open felt suitcase with travel tags. Generous negative space (keep ~35%+ of the canvas empty); the subject is large and confident, ~50–70% of the frame.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot — a plump cat-like blob figure built from layered hand-cut felt pieces (a smooth, rounded dome body with two tiny triangular felt ears on top), two printed dot eyes, blank deadpan (no eyebrows, no mouth), small stubby felt arms and legs; the ONLY accent-colored part is the tail (a small curved felt tail at the back) which uses this palette's accent. The mascot is itself built of stacked hand-cut felt layers exactly like every other felt piece in the scene. It MUST perform the move, not decorate. In a light palette the body felt is pale/oat and the eyes are structure-ink dots.

LINE LANGUAGE: build EVERYTHING — mascot, objects, props — from layered hand-cut FELT pieces with soft rounded edges and a visible fuzzy nap; NO drawn outlines — shapes separate by flat felt color, by the soft drop shadow where one felt layer sits on another, and by occasional simple stitch dashes; the mascot's body itself reads as stacked felt layers (rows of feathers, quills, petals, or tufts), built exactly like every other felt piece in the scene.

STYLE: LAYERED FELT-CRAFT diorama — matte wool-felt cutouts stacked in shallow layers on a felt ground, soft fuzzy fiber texture on every surface, gentle soft drop shadows between stacked layers, slightly imperfect hand-cut edges and small handmade misalignment; NOT glossy, NOT a 3D render, no plastic sheen, no photorealistic depth-of-field, no gradients within a piece (one flat felt color per shape).

PALETTE: a soft felt ground #e7e2d2 breathing through. Layered felt in a small craft color set warm-brown #9a7b5a / sage #8fa682 / dusty-blue #8fa6b0 / oat #c9bfa6 / clay #c08a6e; structure-ink #3a352e only for the eyes, nose, mouth and fine detail. Accent #d9482e used sparingly — the character's one focal accent part + at most 1–2 elements. One flat felt color per shape; soft drop shadows only between stacked layers.

LABELS: exactly 2 short hand-lettered English labels — {"VISITED", "WORLD"} — in the structure-ink color placed directly on the bare felt ground; slightly irregular, stitched look. Never put label text on a colored fill. No title bar, no type label, no logo.
